dialogueChunks.intro: The situation has changed, {player_name}. And not for the better.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The {enemy_type} have split the hostages into two groups and moved them to separate locations. {target_npc}'s message says they're being forced to harvest {quest_item}. One group was taken deeper {location_hint}. Hitting one site will alert the other. Our efforts to {quest_action} just doubled in complexity.
dialogueChunks.outro: Two locations, limited time, and {quest_stakes} at risk if they suspect a rescue. The pressure just doubled.

playerResponses.text: Split groups means I need to hit hard and fast. Give me the locations.
Two sites, one rescuer? The odds aren't great, but the hostages don't have better options.
Splitting the hostages means they're prepared for a rescue. This is a trap.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, they took another one. While you were tracking the first group, they circled back.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_threat} snatched the blacksmith's apprentice while locking up the forge. {target_npc} found a note scratched into the forge door: coordinates leading {location_hint}. Either they're taunting us or it's a genuine clue. We need you to investigate and gather {quest_item} to equip the militia. Our efforts to {quest_action} need to address the source, not just the symptoms.
dialogueChunks.outro: They're hunting us in our own home. We need to turn the tables before {quest_stakes} grows worse.

playerResponses.text: A taunting message means they're overconfident. I'll use that against them.
More abductions while I'm out searching? We need to solve this at the source.
They can grab people from inside the settlement walls? Nothing I do will be enough.

dialogueChunks.intro: One of the hostages escaped, {player_name}. Barely alive, but they brought crucial information.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The escapee says the {enemy_type} have a tunnel network {location_hint} connecting several holding sites. {target_npc} and the other captives are in the deepest chamber, behind three guard posts. The tunnels are rigged with crude alarms. We need {quest_item} to create a diversion at the main entrance while you slip in through the drainage vent to {quest_action}.
dialogueChunks.outro: We have a way in, but it's narrow, dangerous, and leaves no room for error. {quest_stakes} depend on precision.

playerResponses.text: The drainage vent. A diversion at the front. And no room for error. Let's do it.
Tunnel networks and alarm systems? At least now we know what we're dealing with.
Booby-trapped tunnels and a single narrow entrance? That's a death sentence.

dialogueChunks.plotStep: We gathered some {quest_item} for the ransom, but it's barely half what they demanded. {target_npc} smuggled out word that the {enemy_type} are arguing among themselves: some want to execute the hostages, others want to keep them as workers. The division might be our best chance to {quest_action}. If you can reach {quest_focus} {location_hint} during the argument and strike while they're distracted, we might pull this off.
dialogueChunks.outro: An enemy divided is an enemy vulnerable. But {quest_stakes} depend on us acting before they resolve their debate.

playerResponses.text: Internal division is an opportunity. I'll strike before they resolve it.
Racing against a debate among monsters. Strange days. Give me the approach route.
Gambling on an internal dispute? Too many unknowns.
